steps.title: Identify where the minimum occurs
steps.content: The function $f(x) = (x-2)(x+15)$ is a quadratic in factored form. The leading coefficient is positive (1), so the parabola opens upward. This means the function reaches its minimum at the vertex, not at the roots.

steps.title: Calculate the vertex x-coordinate using the axis of symmetry
steps.content:
For a parabola with roots 2 and -15, the vertex lies on the axis of symmetry at the midpoint of these roots:

$$x = \frac{2 + (-15)}{2} = \frac{-13}{2}$$

The function reaches its minimum at $x = -\frac{13}{2}$.
